title: RPYR-0008 (lead) — `new_invalid_state_error` downcasts a called-type result: `Future.result()` on a pending future with a monkeypatched `InvalidStateError` (`_asyncio.rs:2737`)
one_line_repro: Future.result() on a pending future after monkeypatching asyncio.exceptions.InvalidStateError to a non-exception factory
found_by: git-history-analyzer (similar-bug; internal-tier helper the scanner suppresses)
toolkit_class: python-reachable-panic (cross-function — internal-tier helper reached from a py method)
cpython_behavior: raises InvalidStateError (the standard 'Result is not set' / 'not ready' exception)
guarded_twin: crates/stdlib/src/_asyncio.rs — the function is defensive on every OTHER branch: Err(_) => vm.new_runtime_error(...), missing-attr => vm.new_runtime_error(...); only the success branch .unwrap()s the downcast
notes: SAME SHAPE AS RPYR-0003 (a downcast of an exc_type.call() result), and a cross-function reachability case the panic SCANNER suppresses (new_invalid_state_error is an internal-tier free helper, not #[pyfunction]/#[pymethod]) — but it is reachable via Future.result()/exception() on a pending future. The history agent flagged it by pattern-matching the same downcast-of-called-type shape. Recorded as a LEAD: the code path is reasoned but a working end-to-end trigger (routing through the pending-future 'not ready' path with a monkeypatched InvalidStateError) has not been isolated. TODO before promoting to confirmed: build the trigger and capture the panic. Not contributed to known_panics.tsv until reproduced.
